sections.description: Standalone workshop inventory and production system: raw materials, multi-level recipes, production runs, an auditable movement ledger, a phone PWA, and one-way stock push to a WooCommerce shop via VGC Stock Bridge.
sections.changelog:
1.27.0

  • Record what a partner sells from consignment stock, right on the order. Consignment orders gain a Sales from sent stock section with a Record sale button that opens a slide-in panel: each unsold item you sent has a plus/minus stepper (capped at what is still unsold) and a Vendor pays amount that fills from the agreed price but can be overridden. Enter the actual amount paid and an invoice, save, and the sold quantities update, a payment is logged, and stock reconciles automatically. Recorded sales show as a log you can tap to edit or remove, cleanly reversing on edit/delete.

1.26.0

  • Groundwork for recording consignment sales - nothing changes on screen yet. Adds the behind-the-scenes storage and calculations for recording each sale a partner makes from stock left on consignment (items, quantity, invoice, amount actually paid): a recorded sale adjusts sold quantities, logs the payment, and reconciles stock automatically, and editing or removing it cleanly reverses. The recording screen arrives next update.

1.25.0

  • The Purchase screen now matches the new Order look. A purchase opens with the same document header (number, vendor, Bought/Consignment) and a row of live summary tiles (Cost, VAT, a highlighted We owe them, Paid, Balance, Retail value, Potential profit, Still-held) at the top. Behaviour unchanged — only the layout is refreshed.

1.24.0

  • The Order screen has a new look — first step of the redesign. An order opens with a clean document header and a row of live summary tiles (Net, VAT, a highlighted Total, Paid, Balance, Margin, Still-out). Display pieces on loan sit in their own blue panel. Edits, totals, payments and saving are unchanged — only the layout is refreshed.

1.23.0

  • Groundwork for the redesign — nothing changes on screen yet. Adds the shared building blocks the upcoming visual refresh needs (badges/labels, summary tiles, a document header, line cells, a slide-in drawer, new icons), not wired into any screen yet, so the app looks and behaves exactly as before. Keeps each redesign step small and safe.

1.22.2

  • Fix: every item stuck as a material and role changes would not save. The database column for the product/material flag (and newer price/store columns) was never created on some sites because an earlier CREATE TABLE contained SQL comments WordPress cannot parse. This adds any missing columns directly, then sorts items — sellable, priced or made-from-recipe become products; the rest stay materials. Role changes now stick.

1.22.1

  • Fix: items looked marked as both a product and a material. The small how-its-made tag still said Product/Material, clashing with the new role. It now reads Made or Bought. This also resolves the impression that changing an item role did not save — it did; the leftover tag just never changed.

1.22.0

  • Internal only — nothing changes in how the app works. The REST API code (one 3,200-line file) was reorganised into per-resource modules so future features are quicker and safer to add. Verified route-for-route identical to 1.21.0.

1.21.0

  • Materials and Products are now separate menu entries instead of one Items list. Each opens to its own list; a new item created from one lands on that side.
  • A clear mark on every item: a product for sale or a material/ingredient — independent of whether it is bought in or made from a recipe.
  • Existing items were sorted automatically (sellable-to-shop or priced items became products); change any on its edit screen. The old Type field is now How it is obtained.

1.20.1

  • Fix: editing a product errored with "i is not defined" and showed a blank card. A pricing helper referenced the item from the wrong scope. Fixed and verified by rendering the edit screen in a browser for both recipe and bought items.

1.20.0

  • Three separate item prices: Manufacturing cost (recipe or manual), Retail sale price, and a standard B2B price. No longer one shared field, so a cost can never be mistaken for a price.
  • Margin shown for each selling price over the manufacturing cost — money and percent, live in the form and on the item page.
  • Fixed the Bulgarian label that caused the confusion: the cost field read "Единична цена" (unit price); now "Себестойност" (cost).
  • Existing values untouched. If your cost field actually holds sale prices, they can be moved to Retail price in bulk for products on request.

1.19.0

  • Products can be stocked at your physical store. Tick it on an item, then Distribute how many units are on the shop floor; the rest stays at the workshop. Moving to the store does NOT change total stock — the goods are still yours, just on a different shelf.
  • Record a store sale and the units leave the shelf and your stock together. If stock falls for any other reason the store allocation trims itself. Every move is written to its own ledger.
  • Recipe products can take a manual cost — override the bill-of-materials cost by ticking a box.

1.18.0

  • Every partner can now trade in both directions. Declaring a partner a customer or a maker used to hide half the app from them — a shop you supply could not also sell you goods. Direction belongs to the document, not the partner.
  • The Type dropdown is gone — what a partner does is read off their real documents and shown as a label (Buys from us / Supplies us / both).
  • Both sides on one screen: a partner page shows their Orders and Purchases, with New order and New purchase side by side. What you owe them now counts open purchases too.

1.17.0

  • New: Purchases — the inbound mirror of an order. One editable document per vendor delivery, with a payment trail and completion that reopens.
  • Buy outright or consignment. Consignment goods sit in the held bucket, not your stock, and you owe only for what sells. Switch the mode to Buy to keep them — the ledgers work out the difference.
  • Vendors not registered for VAT charge a final price: no VAT in it, nothing to reclaim, counted as cost in full.
  • Markup → sell price, per line or for the whole delivery, showing net and shelf price. Markup is % of cost, so the margin it equals is shown too (20% markup = 16.7% margin).
  • Create items inline while receiving. Prices are never pushed to the shop.

1.16.1

  • The partner statement stat row is readable again. Its tiles picked up an old, unrelated .vgc-sm-stat rule from a replaced component, laying each tile out as a flex row — figure and caption shoved to opposite ends instead of stacked. Dead rule removed.
  • Caption on top, figure underneath.
  • On desktop the row is now always exactly two rows — the columns are computed from the tile count, which changes with the data.

1.16.0

  • The "prices include VAT" tick now covers the whole row, cost included. It only governed the sale price, so a gross-typed cost was subtracted at full value from a VAT-stripped price — understating every margin on a gross order. 6 @ 9.70 gross with a 3.00 gross cost reported 30.50; it is 33.50.
  • Both money column headers now state their basis (с ДДС / без ДДС) and follow the tick.
  • Recipe-filled costs are grossed up to match on a gross-typed order.
  • Check any order where you did not type the cost by hand — auto-filled costs were stored net and are now read as gross.

1.15.1

  • Fixed: the partner statement measured margin % against VAT-inclusive turnover while the order screen used net, so the same order showed two different margins. Both now use net.
  • Statement Turnover is now net of VAT and labelled so; new Billed (incl. VAT) and VAT tiles keep the gross figures visible.
  • Translated three column headers still showing in English (Quantity, Unit price, SKU).
1.15.0 Partner statement now leads with real stats: turnover, paid, they owe, overdue, profit with margin %, items sold, documents and average value. 1.14.0 Orders now appear on the partner statement (it only counted stock notes before); statements and order lists show Total and Paid alongside the balance. 1.13.1 HOTFIX: a stray apostrophe in the help text caused a fatal PHP parse error in 1.13.0. 1.13.0 Partial payments recorded one by one with a running Remaining; fix: a paid-up order is no longer marked overdue; orders now listed on the partner screen and counted in what a partner owes. 1.12.0 Display pieces sit in their own section under the order lines — no price, VAT or margin, and excluded from the totals. New "Prices I enter include VAT" toggle above the lines. 1.11.1 Order lines readable: grid layout so captions always align with their fields, sensible column widths (the Note field no longer swallows the row), quantities separated from money, and per-field labels on narrow screens. 1.11.0 Orders: one living, always-editable document per customer order — ordered/sent/sold/returned on the same lines, pay-in-full or pay-as-sold, transport and who pays it, paid/balance/margin, complete and reopen. Stock follows your edits; nothing is locked. 1.10.1 Fix: printing no longer makes the app re-render the page mid-print. 1.10.0 Payment terms per partner with due dates and overdue flags on notes and statements; returns can be refunded (credit back what they owe); display pieces flagged per line instead of needing a separate product; print fixed for long notes (A4 portrait, repeating headers, no clipping). 1.9.0 Stock notes: settable date, "already sent" (no stock deduction), destination location, per-line VAT with net/gross auto-fill and per-line notes, compact one-row line editor; partners can be VAT registered with a VAT number. 1.8.0 Pull products: filter by category and product type; virtual products left unticked by default (needs Stock Bridge 0.4.0). 1.7.4 Pull products paginated 20-at-a-time with Previous/Next (large pages could time out); selection remembered across pages, import all at once. 1.7.3 Fix: Pull products fetches the whole catalogue (was capped at the first 100 products); walks every page with a live count. 1.7.2 Partner name shown in the sticky top bar on the partner page (stays visible while scrolling). 1.7.1 Admin config (connection, translations, team, activity log) grouped under one Settings hub with tabs; Help moved to the bottom of the sidebar. 1.7.0 Four access levels (viewer/operator/manager/admin) enforced server-side; in-app Team screen to assign levels; Activity log of who did what (stock, notes, partners, shop, settings, sign-in/out). 1.6.0 Each partner location carries its own contact (name/role/email/phone); standalone contacts kept as Additional contacts; primary record taken from the primary location contact. 1.5.0 Partner statements (money both ways, net position, date range, CSV); printable notes and statements on your letterhead; completes the consignment feature set. 1.4.0 Publish held stock to the shop on demand; Reconcile from shop books online sales as sold_held notes (FIFO across makers, preview then confirm); shop never offers more held than we hold. 1.3.0 Partners can have multiple locations and contacts (one primary each); existing partners migrated automatically; flat fields kept in step with the primaries. 1.2.0 Inbound stock notes + held bucket (take/purchase/buy-held/return-out/sold-from-held); Held stock report; per-maker "You owe" balances; grouped note-type picker; routed the Out-on-consignment page. 1.1.0 Partners with price lists and balances; stock notes (release, sale report, return, direct sale) with draft/issue/cancel; Out on consignment report; shop stock reduced on release. 1.0.1 Shop stock: photos fixed, category filter, collapsible sidebar categories. 1.0.0 First stable release; in-app wiki (Help). 0.24.0 Editable translations (override any string; survives updates). 0.23.0 Sidebar category shortcuts under Items. 0.22.0 Sorting persists and is available on reports, shop stock and pull. 0.21.0 Full-width desktop layout; sortable items table. 0.20.0 Remove items: archive or permanently delete, with recipe-usage protection. 0.19.0 Pull imports product photos; item detail shows live shop stock. 0.18.0 Pull products from the shop into items. 0.17.0 UI language setting + full Bulgarian translation. 0.16.0 Redesign: warm parchment + terracotta, SVG icons, desktop sidebar + tables, sticky cost panels. 0.15.0 Photos for materials, products and recipes. 0.14.0 User-defined categories for materials and products. 0.13.0 Scrap defective units as part of a production run (net good quantity pushed to shop). 0.12.0 Cache-safe updates, Reports (produced/scrapped/stock on hand + CSV), CSV import, scrap with reason. 0.11.0 SKU generation from name, currency setting (default euro), improved price display. 0.10.0 Recipes can carry non-material costs (labour, overhead, misc). 0.9.1 Produce shows recipe→stock unit conversion; recipe editor shows per-component cost + live total. 0.9.0 Unit conversion for weight and volume (recipes reconcile across compatible units). 0.8.0 Recipe components can be entered per item or per batch. 0.7.1 Production is batch/run-based (enter runs; output = runs x yield). 0.7.0 Costing on Produce screen, cost columns in shop/low-stock views, recipe component unit dropdown, currency setting. 0.6.0 Packaging (receive in cartons/boxes, track unopened packs) and pricing with VAT (net/gross auto-calc, manufactured cost rolled up from recipe). 0.5.1 Item form unit dropdown (pcs, ml, kg, g) with add-new-unit. 0.5.0 Sync client: auto-push produced stock to the shop as atomic deltas, shop-stock dashboard, and front-end connection settings. 0.4.0 REST API + front-end SPA (dashboard, items, recipes, production), barcode scanning, installable PWA with offline write queue. 0.3.0 BOM engine + production runs. 0.2.0 front-end shell + login. 0.1.0 foundation.

sections.description: Companion plugin for VGC Stock Manager. Exposes a tiny, token-authenticated REST surface for reading and adjusting WooCommerce product stock quantities only (never orders or customers).
sections.changelog: 0.4.0 /products gains category/type filters and a virtual flag; new /product-categories endpoint. 0.3.0 /products returns full-size image URL. 0.2.0 GET /products so the backoffice can pull shop products in as items. 0.1.0 Initial release: token auth, ping, read stock, adjust (delta), set (absolute).

sections.description: The AI management layer for WordPress (MCP tools for AI agents).
sections.changelog:
2.13.8

  • Compatibility: tool schemas are now valid for strict MCP clients (Claude Desktop 1.18286+) regardless of which MCP adapter serves them.

2.13.7

  • OPcache self-heal: automatically reset OPcache after plugin installs/updates so the MCP connector never regresses to stale code.

2.13.6

  • Connect page now stores, lists and revokes connections (previously created credentials were saved but never shown).

2.13.5

  • Fix Claude Desktop 1.17377+ tools/list rejection (properties emitted as {} not []).

2.13.4

  • Fix extension subpage toolbar spacing (no longer flush to card edges).

2.13.3

  • Fix Extensions card layout.

sections.description: Self-hosted WooCommerce newsletter - wp_mail sending, open/click tracking, subscribe form and checkout opt-in. No third-party services.
sections.changelog:
0.4.4

  • Per-list signup form: add a group attribute to the shortcode, e.g. [vgc_newsletter_form group="Workshops"], to subscribe visitors to a specific list. The group is created automatically if it does not exist.

0.4.3

  • Filter subscribers by products purchased: search by product name, or filter to buyers of a product category or product tag (paid orders). Combines with the search/status/group filters and is kept across sorting, pagination and bulk actions.

0.4.2

  • The plugin now shows its branded icon on the Updates screen and the plugin details modal, instead of the generic placeholder.

0.4.1

  • Subscribers page: search by email/name, filter by status, sortable columns (email, status, orders, spent, avg/order, added) and pagination (25 per page). Bulk actions keep the current view.
  • Removed the order/spend totals above the subscriber list; only the total customer count remains. Per-subscriber stats are unchanged.

0.4.0

  • Subscriber groups: create and delete groups, bulk add/remove selected subscribers, filter the list by group, and quick-select a group on the Send recipient picker.
  • WooCommerce spend stats: each subscriber shows their number of paid orders, total spent and average per order (HPOS-aware); the current view/group shows combined totals.

0.3.2

  • Branded plugin icon: the wp-admin menu icon is now the VGC mark - an envelope whose flap forms a gold "V" on blue.

0.3.1

  • Assemble emails from saved blocks: a block picker on the Send screen (grouped by category) inserts a saved building block into the composer at the cursor, updating the live preview. Build a newsletter fast — header, product blocks, footer — then edit.

0.3.0

  • Building blocks: a new Blocks section to save reusable HTML elements (headers, footers, product blocks, content sections, buttons, dividers) by category, with a syntax-highlighted editor and live preview. Groundwork for assembling emails from saved blocks in a future update.

0.2.9

  • Syntax-highlighted HTML editor on the Send screen (WordPress core CodeMirror): color-coded tags, attributes and embedded CSS/JS. No third-party libraries; falls back to a plain textarea if syntax highlighting is disabled in your profile.

0.2.8

  • Side-by-side live email preview on the Send screen: HTML editor and rendered preview shown in two columns, updating live (debounced) as you type. Desktop/mobile width toggle.

0.2.7

  • Live email preview on the Send screen: see the newsletter exactly as recipients will (tokens filled in, unsubscribe footer added) before sending, in a sandboxed iframe with desktop/mobile width toggle.

0.2.6

  • Delete reports: bulk delete on the Reports list and a Delete report button per campaign. Removes the campaign and all its opens, clicks and purchase records; already-sent emails and WooCommerce orders are untouched.

0.2.5

  • Campaign report now lists every recipient with their delivery status and per-newsletter actions (opens, clicks, purchases + revenue).
  • Filter tabs: All / Opened / Clicked / Purchased / Not opened; unsubscribed recipients flagged.

0.2.4

  • WooCommerce purchase attribution: a tracked-link click credits any resulting paid order (and its value) to the campaign, last-click within a configurable window.
  • Reports: new Orders and Revenue columns, tiles, and an attributed-orders list per campaign.
  • Settings: purchase-tracking toggle and attribution window (days).

0.2.3

  • Rebranded as VGC - Newsletter with the self-hosted updater (native WordPress "Update now").
  • WooCommerce checkout opt-in: selectable location via standard checkout hooks, plus a custom CSS class.
  • Per-campaign reports: opens, clicks, unsubscribes, clicked-link breakdown, send-time failures.
  • Public [vgc_newsletter_form] subscribe form; unique per-subscriber unsubscribe links and tokens.
